summary: README-only documentation cleanup that reformatted the project overview, build requirements, compiler download instructions, built DOL metadata, and contribution links. The PR added a Discord badge, reference-style Markdown links, clearer sectioning, and explicit shell snippets for Python3, GCC, and make. No code, decompilation symbols, matching logic, or build behavior changed.
what_changed: Reformatted the README heading from a plain title into a title with a Discord badge reference.
Reworded the project description as a WIP decompilation of the US version of Super Smash Bros Melee.
Replaced the old diff-style INFORMATION block with a styled Important section explaining that the built DOL can be shifted and code may be added or removed for modding or research.
Split built DOL information into a dedicated Built DOL section with version 1.02, file main.dol, and SHA1 08e0bf20134dfcb260699671004527b2d6bb1a45.
Renamed Required tools to Requirements and expanded tool entries for DevKitPro, Python3, and GCC with Markdown links and pacman install snippets where applicable.
Expanded build instructions from two steps to three: download GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip, extract contents to /tools/mwcc_compiler/, then run make.
Moved raw URLs to reference-style Markdown definitions for DevKitPro, Python3, GCC, GC_WII_COMPILERS.zip, Discord Badge, and Discord invite.
Reformatted the Contributions section to state that contributions and pull requests are welcome.
smart_moves: Kept all substantive onboarding facts visible: supported DOL version, main.dol SHA, required tools, compiler archive location, extraction path, and make command.
Converted raw URLs into reference-style Markdown links, making the rendered README cleaner and easier to maintain.
Added shell code blocks for commands, which improves copyability for new contributors setting up the decompilation environment.
Separated warning, build metadata, requirements, instructions, and contributions into clearer sections for first-time project users.
decomp_lessons: README updates for a decompilation project should preserve exact build-identifying metadata such as DOL version, filename, and SHA1, because contributors rely on these to verify they are targeting the correct binary.
Onboarding documentation should explicitly state where proprietary/compiler toolchains are expected to be placed, here /tools/mwcc_compiler/, to reduce setup ambiguity.
Even cosmetic documentation changes can matter for decomp work when they make required toolchain setup and reproducible build commands easier to find.
No lessons about function matching, symbol naming, struct layout, or assembly translation can be inferred from this PR because it only changed README.md.
naming_conventions: Uses README section labels: Important, Built DOL, Building, Requirements, Instructions, Contributions.
Documents the built artifact as Version 1.02, File main.dol, and SHA 08e0bf20134dfcb260699671004527b2d6bb1a45.
Uses reference-style link labels: DevKitPro, Python3, GCC, GC, Discord Badge, Discord.
review_feedback: No review comments or issue comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one review recorded, but no review body or inline feedback was included in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later README changes that may have corrected or further refined build setup instructions.
Search for PRs that update compiler acquisition or /tools/mwcc_compiler/ setup.
Search for documentation PRs that mention shifted DOL behavior or reproducible SHA verification.
